news.title: Maharashtra: TDR emerges as new age currency for infrastructure works
news.para: Besa-Pipla Nagar Panchayat is piloting a TDR-based infrastructure model, sparking debate on its potential as a new currency for civic development. This innovative approach allows developers to fund projects like roads and drainage in exchange for development rights, reducing direct financial outlay for local authorities and offering a path to accelerate growth amid funding constraints.

news.title: Maharashtra to issue free rural property cards; sanad fees waived
news.para: Maharashtra government will provide free property cards and waive sanad fees for rural residents under the Centre's Swamitva scheme. This initiative aims to simplify ownership rights, reduce property disputes, and accelerate the settlement of property records across the state. The decision follows extensive digital land mapping and property measurement exercises.

news.title: Bengaluru owners left high and dry after rental promises fall flat
news.para: Property owners are reporting significant delays in receiving rental income from Jones Asset Management, with some facing non-payment for up to eight months. These issues have led to disputes over agreements and concerns about transparency. The situation highlights a growing problem, prompting warnings for both owners and tenants to exercise caution with intermediaries.

news.title: Hyderabad's western corridor may see spike in property registration rates
news.para: Telangana government is considering a significant increase in property registration rates, potentially quadrupling them in Hyderabad's upscale western corridor. Proposed revisions in areas like Raidurg and Gachibowli could see rates jump from around Rs 13 crore to Rs 55 crore per acre. This move aims to bridge the gap between market prices and current registration values, potentially boosting state revenue.

news.title: Over 110 developers get Odisha RERA notices over missing progress reports
news.para: Odisha Real Estate Regulatory Authority (Orera) has issued show-cause notices to 113 developers for failing to submit mandatory quarterly progress reports. Non-compliance, a violation of Rera provisions, can lead to penalties up to 5% of project cost, formal inquiries, and potential suspension or revocation of registration.

news.title: Dalmia Bharat to acquire JAL's cement assets in ₹2,850 crore deal after Adani takeover
news.para: Dalmia Bharat's subsidiary will acquire cement assets from debt-ridden Jaiprakash Associates for Rs 2,850 crore. This strategic acquisition will boost Dalmia Bharat's cement capacity to 54.7 MTPA, with further expansion planned. The deal, expected to finalize within two weeks, aims to diversify markets and reduce regional volatility.

news.title: Kolte-Patil Developers posts net loss ₹38.08 crore in FY26
news.para: Kolte-Patil Developers reported a net consolidated loss of ₹38.08 crore for FY26, a significant downturn from a profit of ₹109.33 crore in FY25. The company's total income also saw a substantial decline of 54.50% to ₹802.50 crore. The board has approved a scheme of amalgamation for its wholly-owned subsidiaries.

news.title: Century Plyboards' net profit rises 44.20% to ₹268.33 crore in FY26
news.para: Century Plyboards reported a significant 44.20% growth in net consolidated profit for FY26, reaching ₹268.33 crore. The company also announced plans to expand its MDF and particle board capacities, investing substantially over the next few years to meet rising demand and improve operational efficiency.

news.title: Ashiana Housing to invest ₹186 crore in first phase of Jaipur project
news.para: Ashiana Housing has announced the launch of the first phase of Ashiana Oma, a residential project in Mansarovar Extension, Jaipur. The first phase will comprise 280 units and involve an estimated investment of ₹186.78 crore, while the overall project is planned across 10.35 acres with 490 units and an estimated project cost of ₹558.60 crore.

news.title: 99acres reports loss before tax of ₹59.15 crore in FY26
news.para: Info Edge's 99acres division reported a FY26 pre-tax loss of ₹59.15 crore, despite an 18.81% revenue jump to ₹488.05 crore. Meanwhile, Info Edge's consolidated net profit surged 34.58% to ₹1,762.84 crore in FY26, with total income rising 10.72% to ₹4,343.28 crore. The board proposed a ₹3.60 dividend.

news.title: India's proptech intelligence layer: Aurum Analytica's AI-first bet starts paying off, reports strong growth in FY26
news.para: As the real estate sector embraces technology at an unprecedented pace, Aurum Analytica is leading the charge — turning artificial intelligence from a buzzword into measurable business outcomes for India's top developers. We caught up with Prakash Tejwani (CEO & Founder), Sahil R. (CBO & Co-Founder) and Vishal Sharma (CTO & Co-Founder) of Aurum Analytica, to understand what made FY 2025–26 a landmark year for the company and where the journey goes from here.

news.title: Single window clearances planned for New Nagpur
news.para: Nagpur Metropolitan Region Development Authority (NMRDA) has partnered with the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India (ICAI) to develop a single window clearance system for its ambitious New Nagpur Business District project. This collaboration aims to enhance ease of doing business and investor facilitation through transparent, technology-driven mechanisms.

news.title: Water scarcity hits premium townships across Indore
news.para: Indore's upscale colonies are grappling with a severe water crisis as borewells dry up and Narmada supply proves insufficient. Residents are now heavily reliant on costly private water tankers, leading to increased maintenance charges and disrupted daily routines. The situation is expected to worsen as summer temperatures rise.

news.title: Samhi Hotels reports net profit of ₹566.54 crore in FY26
news.para: Samhi Hotels reported a significant jump in net profit to ₹566.54 crore for FY26, with total income rising 12.33%. The company highlighted strong revenue growth despite disruptions and a comfortable Net Debt to EBITDA ratio of ~3x. Key developments include a new Marriott hotel in Sriperumbudur and strategic investments.

news.title: Seven Pune civic body colonies up for redevelopment, residents get notices to vacate
news.para: Pune Municipal Corporation has issued notices to occupants of seven dilapidated housing complexes, urging them to vacate for redevelopment. Approximately 1,900 flats are in poor condition, with 288 prioritized for demolition and rebuilding. The project aims to provide tenants with new 550 sqft properties on a rental basis after reconstruction.

news.title: Mumbai: Commercial tower surfaces in DRP plan for Mulund salt pans
news.para: Navbharat Mega Developers, the special purpose vehicle for the Dharavi Redevelopment Project, has submitted a plan to develop Mulund, Bhandup, and Kanjurmarg salt pans. The proposal includes 18 free-sale and one commercial building on Mulund's salt pan, alongside rehabilitation towers.

news.title: Equity Residential, AvalonBay to merge in mega US real estate deal
news.para: Equity Residential and AvalonBay Communities are merging in an all-stock deal. This creates a housing rental giant valued at 69 billion dollars. The combined entity will be the largest publicly traded apartment REIT in the US. This merger aims to boost investment in AI technology. Synergies are expected to reduce costs and improve margins.
